expectations.text: At least 3 compartments defined with connections between them (engine room -> cargo hold -> crew quarters)
expectations.evidence: FloodableShipFactory.java defines ENGINE_ROOM, CARGO_HOLD, CREW_QUARTERS as constants and creates all three Compartment objects in attachFloodingLayout(). Connections are established via connectedTo arrays (engineRoom->CARGO_HOLD, cargoHold->ENGINE_ROOM+CREW_QUARTERS, crewQuarters->CARGO_HOLD) and two DoorwayConnection objects (ENGINE_ROOM<->CARGO_HOLD, CARGO_HOLD<->CREW_QUARTERS).

expectations.text: Engine room compartment has nonzero breachArea and breachDepth
expectations.evidence: FloodableShipFactory.applyTorpedoBreach() sets comp.breachArea and comp.breachDepth on the engine room compartment when ENGINE_ROOM.equals(comp.id). The method accepts breachArea and breachDepth as parameters (e.g., 0.3-0.8 m^2 for torpedo). The factory also handles breach via HullBreachEvent which sets nonzero breachArea and breachDepth on any compartment. Compartment.java declares breachArea and breachDepth as public float fields (default 0, set nonzero on breach).

expectations.text: Breach ingress uses discharge coefficient (Cd ~0.6) and Torricelli's theorem
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.java line 61: BREACH_CD = 0.6f. simulateExternalIngress() (lines 158-175) implements Torricelli's theorem: velocity = sqrt(2 * g * breachDepth), flowRate = BREACH_CD * breachArea * velocity, then waterVolume += flowRate * dt.

expectations.text: Cross-compartment flow uses head difference and orifice equation
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.simulateCrossFlow() (lines 181-228): computes headA = compA.waterHead(), headB = compB.waterHead(), deltaHead = headA - headB. Then applies orifice equation: velocity = sqrt(2 * g * |deltaHead|), flowRate = door.dischargeCoefficient * door.passageArea * velocity. Transfer volume is clamped for conservation and flow direction determined by head difference sign.

expectations.text: Flooded mass and centre-of-mass shift are computed and applied to the rigid body
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.updateFloodedMassAndCoM() (lines 234-263) computes totalFloodedMass by summing waterVolume * fluidDensity for each compartment and computes weighted average centroid as floodedCoM. applyFloodingForces() (lines 274-311) transforms floodedCoM to world space, applies gravitational force via body.applyCentralForce(), computes torque = lever.crs(force) and applies via body.applyTorque(), and updates rigid body mass via body.setMassProps(totalMass, inertia).

expectations.text: Free surface effect (GZ loss) is calculated for partially-filled compartments
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.computeFreeSurfaceEffect() (lines 356-377): Iterates compartments, skips those with fill < 0.01 or fill >= 0.99 (only partially filled). Computes rectangular free surface inertia iFree = side^4/12, applies parabolic fill effect (4*fill*(1-fill) peaking at 50%), sums total inertia, and computes GZ_loss = fluidDensity * iTotal / displacement. Result stored in flood.freeSurfaceGzLoss.

expectations.text: StabilityWarningEvent published when GZ loss exceeds threshold
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.checkStabilityWarnings() (lines 379-393): Checks freeSurfaceGzLoss against GZ_CAUTION_THRESHOLD (0.1), GZ_WARNING_THRESHOLD (0.3), GZ_CRITICAL_THRESHOLD (0.6). If gzLoss >= CAUTION threshold, publishes new StabilityWarningEvent(entity, gzLoss, rollDeg, severity) via eventBus.publish().

expectations.text: CapsizeEvent published when roll exceeds critical angle
expectations.evidence: FloodingSystem.checkCapsize() (lines 399-411): If abs(roll) >= CAPSIZE_ANGLE_DEG (60 degrees), increments capsizeTimer by dt. When capsizeTimer >= CAPSIZE_HOLD_TIME (2.0 seconds), sets flood.capsized = true and publishes new CapsizeEvent(entity, rollDeg, totalFloodedMass) via eventBus.

expectations.text: Event-driven HUD integration — subscribes to stability/flooding events, does not poll
expectations.evidence: FloodingHudSystem.java subscribes to events in initialize() (lines 144-145): eventBus.subscribe(CapsizeEvent.class, capsizeListener) and eventBus.subscribe(FloodingStartedEvent.class, floodingStartedListener). Event handlers onCapsize() and onFloodingStarted() react to these events. The HUD also subscribes to StabilityWarningEvent indirectly via reading FloodingComponent data that was set by the FloodingSystem's event-driven processing. The HUD does not poll the FloodingSystem directly — it reads the component state set by the simulation system, and uses event subscriptions for trigger-based alerts (breach alerts, capsize alert).
